input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for their project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to first understand the project structure, then set up and run tests with coverage, and finally analyze the results.

Let me start by exploring the project structure to understand what we're working with.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage via coverlet

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with code coverage via coverlet

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage enabled using coverlet, then analyze the results.

The project:
- .NET 9.0 solution with MSTest and coverlet.collector
- Main project has `StudentService.cs` with 4 methods: `Enroll`, `CalculateGpa`, `FindById`, `Search`
- Test project has tests for `Enroll` (2 tests), `FindById` (2 tests), `Search` (1 test), but NO tests for `CalculateGpa`

Let me run the tests with coverage collection.

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage via coverlet

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-364791ede3f64c0597646da9d9758d73 && dotnet test --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --results-directory ./TestResults -- DataCollectionRunSettings.DataCollectors.DataCollector.Configuration.Format=cobertura 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with code coverage via coverlet", "initial_wait": 60}
